name: Coll de Pal and Via del Nicolau Loop
name_original: Vía del Nicolau y Coll de Pal - circular gravel por el Parque Natural del Cadí-Moixeró
description: This difficult 25.0-mile touring cycling loop combines the challenging Coll de Pal ascent with the gentle Via del Nicolau rail-trail, gainin
region_intro: Hiking around Guardiola De Berguedà explores a landscape characterized by the pre-Pyrenean mountains, featuring a mix of forested valleys, river paths, and higher mountain terrain. The area includes significant elevation changes, leading to various viewpoints and natural features. This region provides a diverse environment for outdoor activities, with routes suitable for different experience levels. Best hiking trails around Guardiola De Berguedà The most popular hiking route is La Tossa Viewpoint – Niu de l'Àliga Refuge loop from Xalet Refugi de Coll de Pal, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers expansive views from La Tossa and passes a mountain refuge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Nicolau Trail Tunnel 2 – Nicolau Suspension Bridge loop from Guardiola de Berguedà, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.6 km) path. This trail features unique elements like old railway tunnels and a suspension bridge over a river. Local hikers also love the Penyes Altes del Moixeró – Moixeró Summit loop from Gréixer, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through high mountain terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 46 minutes. This difficult route involves a significant ascent to a prominent summit. Hiking around Guardiola De Berguedà is defined by mountainous terrain, forested valleys, and river paths. The network offers options for both easy hikes and more challenging ascents to higher peaks. The routes in Guardiola De Berguedà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1400 hikers have used komoot to explore Guardiola De Berguedà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Guardiola De Berguedà?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails in the Guardiola De Berguedà area, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents. The region is characterized by pre-Pyrenean mountains, forested valleys, and river paths.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Guardiola De Berguedà offers 5 easy hikes. While specific family-friendly designations aren't always available, routes with lower difficulty and shorter distances are generally suitable. For example, the area features paths through forested valleys that are less strenuous.

Q: What kind of challenging hikes can I find in this region?
A: For experienced hikers, there are 12 difficult routes available. These often involve significant elevation changes and higher mountain terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3982287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyes Altes del Moixeró – Moixeró Summit loop from Gréixer&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.6 km long and involves a substantial ascent to a prominent summit.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Guardiola De Berguedà?
A: Many of the trails in Guardiola De Berguedà are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10473359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tossa Viewpoint – Niu de l'Àliga Refuge loop from Xalet Refugi de Coll de Pal&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route offering expansive views.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see while hiking?
A: The region boasts several notable attractions. You can explore mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/578909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Merolla&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1139176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de Toses Pass&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/949301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lluís Estasen Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6315063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de n'Hug&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4863016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queralt Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant religious building in the area.

Q: Can I find hikes that include unique features like tunnels or bridges?
A: Yes, some trails offer distinctive features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38188635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nicolau Trail Tunnel 2 – Nicolau Suspension Bridge loop from Guardiola de Berguedà&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, featuring old railway tunnels and a suspension bridge over a river, providing a unique hiking experience.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Guardiola De Berguedà?
A: The trails in Guardiola De Berguedà are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, from forested paths to high mountain views, and the well-maintained routes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by mountain refuges or places to rest?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate mountain refuges. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10473359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tossa Viewpoint – Niu de l'Àliga Refuge loop from Xalet Refugi de Coll de Pal&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Niu de l'Àliga Refuge, offering a potential stop during your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Guardiola De Berguedà?
A: The best time to hike in Guardiola De Berguedà is generally from spring to autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is milder, and the trails are more accessible. Higher altitude routes might still have snow in early spring or late autumn, so checking local conditions is always recommended.

Q: Is it possible to hike in Guardiola De Berguedà during winter?
A: Winter hiking is possible, especially on lower-altitude trails. However, higher mountain routes can be challenging due to snow and ice, requiring appropriate gear like snowshoes or crampons. Always check weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out in winter.

Q: Are there any trails with panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. Given its pre-Pyrenean setting, many trails offer stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10470436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrent de Bosoms – Panoramic View loop from Càmping El Berguedà&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for its scenic vistas, and the La Tossa Viewpoint is known for expansive outlooks.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Guardiola De Berguedà?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for significant elevation changes, it's advisable to wear sturdy hiking boots, layered clothing suitable for changing mountain weather, and carry rain gear. Sun protection, sufficient water, and snacks are also essential for any hike in the region.
tips: The little wooden bridge and tunnels await on the climb out of Bagà, as you follow this old converted railway line along the side of the mountain. Greasy when wet!
Small viewpoint overlooking the south face of the Sierra del Cadí. Very frequented area for walking

This section in the Gabarrós valley on the way to Coll de Pal offers us spectacular mountain landscapes, typical of the Pyrenees. During a large part of the route we pedal along the foot of the Puigllançada mountain, at 2409 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Coll de Pal and Via del Nicolau Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop offers a highly varied experience. The Coll de Pal section features challenging mountain roads with significant elevation gain, winding through dense forests and alpine meadows, offering panoramic views. In contrast, the Via del Nicolau is a restored old railway line, largely flat or gently descending, passing through wooded areas with tunnels and a notable suspension bridge.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The overall loop is considered difficult due to the demanding ascent of Coll de Pal, which requires very good fitness. While the Via del Nicolau section is easy and family-friendly, the Coll de Pal climb involves sustained steep gradients. It's best suited for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally from late March to November. The Coll de Pal section can experience snow and cold temperatures during winter, making the descent particularly challenging. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and stunning scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Coll de Pal and Via del Nicolau Loop?
faqs.answer: The picturesque village of Bagà is an excellent base for this route and the starting point for the Coll de Pal ascent. You can typically find parking within or near Bagà to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle the Coll de Pal and Via del Nicolau Loop itself. However, as parts of the route are within the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró, it's important to respect park regulations regarding nature conservation and responsible recreation.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features. The challenging climb of Coll de Pal offers stunning panoramic views of the Cadí-Moixeró mountains. The Via del Nicolau Trail itself is a highlight, with its historical railway line features, tunnels, and the impressive 35-meter suspension bridge (Passarel·la del Nicolau) over the Bastareny river. Don't miss the Orris Viewpoint and the scenic Gabarrós Valley on the way to Coll de Pal.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with several other well-known paths. You'll find parts of the Camí dels bons homes en BTT, [CIMA BA08] Coll de Pal, Ruta Transpirenaica en BTT (Campodron - Noves de Segre), and the Volta Cadí-Moixeró, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Coll de Pal and Via del Nicolau Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Via del Nicolau section is generally accessible, the challenging nature of the Coll de Pal ascent makes this loop less suitable for cycling with dogs, especially if they are not accustomed to long, strenuous climbs. If you do bring a dog, ensure they are well-trained and capable of the distance and elevation, and always keep them on a leash, especially within the natural park.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the starting point of Bagà is a well-equipped village with various amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options there, making it a convenient place to refuel or stay before and after your ride.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for cycling within the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró?
faqs.answer: As this route passes through the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró, it's crucial to respect park regulations. This includes staying on marked trails, not disturbing wildlife, carrying out all your waste, and being mindful of other users. While cycling is generally permitted on designated routes, always check for any specific restrictions or protected areas.

faqs.question: Given the varied difficulty, how can I best prepare for this loop?
faqs.answer: Preparation is key for this loop. Focus on building your climbing fitness for the Coll de Pal section, as it's a significant physical test. Ensure your touring bicycle is in excellent condition, especially your brakes for the descent. The Via del Nicolau offers a welcome, leisurely contrast, but don't underestimate the overall distance and elevation gain.

waypoints.name: Gabarrós Valley on the way to Coll de Pal
